title: niphobolus spissus
intro: Niphobolus spissus, also known as the Indian spikenard or Makandi, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. Traditionally, Niphobolus spissus has been used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Niphobolus spissus is often prepared as a herbal tea or powder for medicinal purposes, and can also be used in traditional remedies such as ointments and infusions.
